name: Kutia (Ukrainian Traditional)
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Honey (dissolved in 3/4 cup hot water)
recipeInstructions:
This is the ritual first dish of the 12-course Christmas Eve Supper. The wheat represents the staff of life, and the honey represents the Spirit of Christ.
1. Dry wheat in a 250-degree oven for one hour, stirring occasionally.
2. Wash the wheat and soak it in cold water overnight.
3. The next morning, bring the wheat to boiling point and simmer for 3-4 hours until the kernels burst open.
4. Scald the poppy seeds and simmer them for 3-5 minutes. Drain and grind twice using the finest blade of a food chopper. Set aside.
5. Combine honey and sugar in hot water. Set aside.
6. After all ingredients have cooled, combine them in one bowl and add the chopped apples (if desired).
7. This dish is to be served cold and will not keep well at room temperature. Store in the refrigerator until ready to serve.
If anyone found this to be interesting, leave a message, and I can post more of the traditional Ukrainian dishes.
